key_insight: A Monte Carlo simulation framework can convert published aggregate trial data into patient-level risk predictions for rare cancers, demonstrated in Ewing sarcoma.
clinical_relevance: It may improve survivorship surveillance, risk stratification, and future trial design when individual-level data are scarce.
method: Built a six-stage discrete-event Monte Carlo model.
Integrated genetic risk, serial biomarker dynamics, ctDNA-MRD, and competing mortality risks.
Added long-term toxicity modules across five organ systems after chemo/radiation exposure.
evidence_signal: In validation against published Ewing sarcoma trial data from more than 3,400 patients, the model achieved 3.2% mean absolute error across 23 efficacy endpoints and stayed within published confidence intervals for all 20 toxicity endpoints.
watch_item: The framework is validated on published aggregate data, so transfer to other rare cancers and clinical decision use still needs prospective confirmation.
paper_title: Multi-Timepoint Risk Stratification in Rare Cancers: A Computational Framework Validated against Published Ewing Sarcoma Trial Data
